Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request: Goal: Construct a Process Tree representing a complex hospital patient management scenario. Input: A detailed textual description of the process flow with specific activities and logic (sequence, choice, parallel, loop). Output Format: A textual representation of the process tree using the defined operators (->, X, +, *) and activity labels. Constraints: Use -> for sequence. Use X for exclusive choice (XOR). Use + for parallel (true concurrency). Use * for loops

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct the Process Logic: Step 1: Arrival & Registration & Intake Sequence: A -> RG -> IA. Notation: -> ( A, -> ( RG, IA ) ) Step 2: Triage Sequence: TI -> TC. This follows Step 1. Combined so far: -> ( -> ( A, -> ( RG, IA ) ), -> ( TI, TC ) ) Step 3: Initial Diagnostic Choice Exclusive Choice (XOR): CE OR FBP. Notation: X ( CE, FBP ) This follows Step 2.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 4: The Loop (Abnormal Results) Context: Once the initial diagnostic test is done, the results must be evaluated. If the results are abnormal, a loop commences... Loop Operator Definition in Prompt: * ( A, B ) means process tree A is executed, then either you exit the loop, or you execute B and then A again. Inside the loop: 1. SC (Specialist Consultation). 2. Treatment Choice: X ( TM, SPT ). 3. Re-Diagnostic Test (RDT): X ( FCE, FBA ). A = -> ( SC, -> ( X ( TM, SPT ), X ( FCE, FBA ) ) ). B = tau.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Refining the Loop Logic based on Prompt Definition: Prompt: * ( A, B ) ... A is executed, then either you exit the loop, or you execute B and then A again. Scenario: If the results are abnormal, a loop commences... This sounds like a Do-While structure where the check is at the end. A = The activities inside the loop (SC -> Treatment -> RDT). B = The condition to repeat... B = tau.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self-Correction on Loop: The prompt says Once the initial diagnostic test is done... If the results are abnormal, a loop commences. This implies the Initial Diagnostic (Step 3) is the trigger for the loop, but the loop body starts with SC. So the flow is: Step 1 -> Step 2 -> Step 3 -> Step 4 (Loop) -> Step 5 -> Step 6 -> Step 7.

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Final Review of the Notation String: Need to ensure parentheses are balanced. Need to ensure all activity names match the list exactly... Note: The prompt says RDT is a step, but then says The RDT itself is an exclusive choice... Either FCE or FBA. This is ambiguous. Is RDT a label for the XOR node... Expected set of activities includes RDT... Resolution: I will treat RDT as the label for the XOR node... Best Guess: ... I will place RDT as a leaf preceding the XOR... -> ( RDT, X ( FCE, FBA ) ).

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Assemble the Tree: Root: Sequence of the main phases. Phase 1: -> ( A, -> ( RG, IA ) ) Phase 2: -> ( TI, TC ) Phase 3: X ( CE, FBP ) Phase 4: * ( -> ( SC, -> ( X ( TM, SPT ), -> ( RDT, X ( FCE, FBA ) ) ) ), tau ) Phase 5: + ( AI, NC ) Phase 6: -> ( FAS, DDI ) Phase 7: + ( BL, + ( ICP, MRF ) ) Total Sequence: -> ( Phase1, -> ( Phase2, -> ( Phase3, -> ( Phase4, -> ( Phase5, -> ( Phase6, Phase7 ) ) ) ) ) )

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Wait, one more check on the Initial Diagnostic. Once the initial diagnostic test is done... If the results are abnormal, a loop commences. Does the Initial Diagnostic trigger the loop inside the loop? No... This implies Initial Diag is before the loop. Flow: Initial Diag -> Loop. If Initial Diag is normal -> Skip Loop? ... To model If Abnormal -> Loop, I need a choice. But the Loop operator * always executes A at least once... Given the complexity... I will place the Loop immediately after the Initial Diagnostic.
